title: Cooling Jars
desc: The service counters rely on shaded jars and damp wraps to keep the lounge's drinks from turning to bathwater.

steps.text:
No one comes to the Mipedim Lounge to be reminded they live in a desert. That requires constant intervention. Beneath the service counters, rows of cooling jars sit in shaded wells wrapped with damp cloth and fed by carefully rationed water drips that keep the evening drinks cool enough to feel luxurious instead of merely safe. When one row warms, patrons notice immediately and act as if civilization itself has been insulted.

The refreshment keeper uncaps a jar, frowns at the temperature, and shuts it again. "Reset the damp wraps on the counter wells if you want the cleaner job," she says. "Or sort the cups and tray sets for the first wave of guests if you prefer to think ahead to the complaints before they happen. Both save me from hearing about warm tea."

options.label: Reset the damp wraps and cooling wells beneath the counter
Sort the cups and tray sets for the opening service

steps.text:
The damp wraps have dried unevenly, which is all it takes to turn a cooling well into an ordinary storage shelf. You strip the old cloths away, soak fresh wraps in the keeper's measured basin, and bind them back around the clay jars so the slow evaporation can do its quiet work again. The difference is not instant, but it is visible. Moisture beads on the jar sides. The clay darkens. The next test cup comes up cool enough to satisfy the keeper's very specific standards.

She pours a little onto the back of her wrist, then nods. "Better. A lounge may survive many things. Lukewarm service is not among them." Around you, the counter line regains its small dignity one sweating jar at a time.

options.label: Hang the used cloths and leave the jars cooling properly

steps.text:
Opening service happens faster than it looks from the cushions. Cups must be matched to trays by size and purpose, date bowls paired with the deeper ceramic sets, and the low mirrored service plates reserved for guests who want to appear to have ordered very little while actually occupying half a table. You sort the stacks accordingly, laying the first-run trays where the servers can reach them without crossing the room like startled birds.

By the time the keeper checks your work, the service counter has become a machine of quiet intention. Nothing clinks. Nothing is out of place. The evening's first trays can leave in clean sequence as soon as the doors admit enough status to justify them. The keeper glances at the neat rows and says, "Good. Now the lounge can offer comfort efficiently, which is the only kind of comfort worth paying for."

options.label: Step back from the counter and let the evening begin
